items.title: Consumer prices rose 3.5% annually in June, less than expected as energy prices eased
items.content: CPI fell 0.4% monthly (biggest drop since April 2020), annual rate 3.5% vs expected 3.8%. Core CPI flat monthly, 2.6% annual vs 2.9% expected. Fed rate hike odds for September dropped from 75% to 63%. Energy index slumped 5.7% monthly.

items.title: US500 fell to 7521 on July 16, losing 0.67%
items.content: S&P 500 fell to 7521 points on July 16, down 0.67%. Up 1.36% over past month and 19.44% year-over-year. Dow set record closing high after soft US jobs data on July 2.

items.title: Dow Closes at Record After Crossing 53000 for First Time
items.content: Dow Jones crossed 53,000 for first time on July 9. Major indexes ended higher as AI-tied stocks rebounded. S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted weekly gains for week ending July 10.

items.title: Dow jumps to record closing high after soft US jobs data
items.content: Wall Street: Dow hit record closing high following softer-than-expected jobs data. Nasdaq down with chip shares amid rotation.

items.title: Big Banks Smash Earnings Records, but 'Tectonic' Risks Loom
items.content: Five largest US banks reported collective $49 billion in profits in Q2 2026, smashing records. JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Wells Fargo all beat expectations despite geopolitical uncertainty from Iran conflict.

items.title: ASML shares fall after hiking sales forecast second time this year on strong AI chip demand
items.content: ASML raised FY2026 guidance to 43-45B euros (from 36-40B). Q2 net sales 9.3B euros vs 8.8B expected. Net profit 2.9B vs 2.6B. Shares surged 115% YTD. Citing AI chip demand driving capacity expansion.

items.title: Chinese AI stock trade remains intact despite 10% correction from jumbo IPO, Fed jitters
items.content: AI growth stocks earnings strong. AI capex may maintain 50% annual compound growth through 2030. AI-pulled demand for domestic semiconductor products is showing. 10% correction from jumbo IPO and Fed concerns.

items.title: China stocks close down on chip profit-taking
items.content: Chinese stocks ended slightly lower amid sell-off in semiconductor shares as investors locked in recent gains. Mainland China stocks closed 0.2% lower at 4,786.78 after China posted slowest quarterly GDP growth since 2022.

items.title: Hang Seng rebounded 0.52% to 24,340.73 on Tuesday
items.content: Hang Seng Index rose 127 points or 0.52% to 24,340.73 on July 14. Earlier fell below 24,000 intraday on Gulf geopolitical risks before recovering. Index tracked higher for four straight sessions to above 24,680.

items.title: US and Iran threaten return to war after fiercest exchange of fire since truce
items.content: US-Iran ceasefire teetering after second round of US retaliatory strikes. Iran wants ships use northern passage in Strait of Hormuz; US sponsors southern route. Trump said ceasefire 'over' at NATO summit. July 9: ceasefire on edge of collapse.

items.title: How the US-Iran ceasefire and MOU broke down - a timeline
items.content: Ceasefire teetered on edge of total collapse July 9 when US completed second round of strikes. Strait of Hormuz navigation remains contested. Iran closed strait again on June 20 citing Israeli violations.

items.title: US proposed export curbs target an already fragile China chip market
items.content: ASML shares fell despite raising guidance after proposed US export curbs targeting China. China stocks closed lower on chip profit-taking. US continues tightening semiconductor export controls to China.
